title: [InstCombine] Simplify commutative compares of symmetric pairs
summary:
The generalization strategy involves identifying a common pattern across multiple test cases where a comparison (fcmp/icmp) operates on two select instructions with swapped operands but the same condition. The key insight is that such comparisons can be simplified by directly comparing the underlying values, eliminating the selects entirely.

The patch implements this by adding a symmetric pair matcher in both ICmp and FCmp handling. When a commutative comparison is detected, it checks if the operands form a symmetric pair (select(cond, A, B) vs select(cond, B, A)). If matched, it replaces the comparison operands with A and B directly, allowing the comparison to be evaluated without the conditional logic.

This transforms patterns like `fcmp oeq (select cond, A, B), (select cond, B, A)` into `fcmp oeq A, B`, which can often be constant-folded or further simplified. The approach works for any commutative comparison predicate (eq/ne for integers, oeq/une/ogt/etc. for floats) and handles both integer and floating-point types uniformly.

The strategy demonstrates how to generalize from a specific optimization case by:
1. Identifying the core structural pattern (symmetric selects)
2. Applying it to all commutative comparisons
3. Implementing it at the appropriate abstraction level in the compiler pipeline
